title: Infertility hormone result review

investigations.text: Defines female, male, and unexplained-factor evaluation, menstrual-pattern context, ovulation confirmation, reproductive hormones, semen analysis, tubal assessment, and uterine-cavity assessment.
investigations.rationale: The exact WHO diagnostic pathway supports recording result source and date, fertility-related symptom context and clinician-reviewed endocrine laboratory results without diagnosing a cause of infertility.
investigations.evidence_extract: Defines female, male, and unexplained-factor evaluation, menstrual-pattern context, ovulation confirmation, reproductive hormones, semen analysis, tubal assessment, and uterine-cavity assessment.

source.title: Guideline for the prevention, diagnosis and treatment of infertility

exact_section.heading: Chapter 5.1–5.3 — female, male, and unexplained infertility diagnostic pathways

treatment_and_management.text: Requires preference-sensitive discussion, psychosocial support, transparent information, and consent-based collaborative decisions.
treatment_and_management.rationale: The exact person-centred section supports patient concerns, questions, clinician-entered plan and arranged follow-up only when discussed; it does not interpret a hormone result or select treatment.
treatment_and_management.evidence_extract: Requires preference-sensitive discussion, psychosocial support, transparent information, and consent-based collaborative decisions.

source.title: Guideline for the prevention, diagnosis and treatment of infertility

exact_section.heading: Chapter 3.3 — diagnosis discussion, psychosocial support, and shared decisions

source_limitations: Specific hormone, value, units, reference range, timing, fertility duration, partner data, clinician interpretation, diagnosis, treatment and referral remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
